Appearances: David Nemecek, via Zoom, for Geode Investments Prime LLC, Geode Investments Beta LLC, Petitioners, and Szu-Yu Wang, Administrator.

Mr. Nemecek reports that Plaintiffs filed a motion in the Civil Action to amend their complaint to substitute Sean Sharma estate as a defendant for Sean Sharma. The hearing on that motion is set for November 2, 2022. If that motion is granted, it will be necessary for creditors to file a petition to appoint counsel for Sean Sharma's estate and therefore requests that the Court lift the stay in this matter to allow him to file that petition if and when it becomes necessary to do so. The court and counsel discuss the dilemma in this matter caused by Geode's status as creditor. Mr. Nemecek suggests appointing outside counsel and refers the court to probate code 9252(c). The court reviews the probate code and suggests either that route or to appoint the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA) which the court has the power to do. The court reminds counsel that if he chooses the court appoint the SFPA, the current Administrator will be suspended, the court will issue an Order to Show Cause RE: Removal and provide notice as required. Counsel advises that he wishes for the court to appoint the SFPA. Accordingly, the court suspends the Administrator in open court.

After additional discussion, the subject matters on today's calendar are continued to Dec-13-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. An Order to Show Cause RE Removal is set for Dec-13-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Appointment of the SFPA is set for the same date.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-22-2020 Continued To Nov-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Nemecek for Geode Investments Beta LLC and Geode Investments Prime LLC.

The Court provided its tentative ruling. The Court stated that to be an administrator of an estate one must be an adult under Probate Code 8402, and that there is an exception for trust companies under Probate Code 300, but it is not aware of any LLC, or any single purpose LLC. The Court also stated that it understands that the purpose of this is that the LLC is a creditor, but it needs an estate representative who can be held accountable. Discussion was held as to what the definition of a person is under the probate code, whether it includes a liability corporation, and whether, or not it would be an ultra-virus. Mr. Nemecek stated that the Heirs are ignoring all communication sent to them, that no one is acting in this estate and that they may have some difficulty getting a Professional who is willing to take this on. Discussion was held about Szu-Yu Wang serving, or one of the Officers of the LLC. The Court provided Mr. Nemecek with the option of continuing this matter for an amendment, or taking the matter off calendar. Mr. Nemecek asked the Court to continue it. The Amended Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to November 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an amended petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Nemecek for Geode Investments Prime LLC and Geode Investments Beta LLC.

The Court noted that the Probate Examiner's notes have not been responded to. Mr. Nemecek stated that the related case is impacting this one, that Mr. Sharma was the Chief Executive Officer of his Client's corporation, that there was a settlement in 2019 for promissory convertible notes, or an acquisition of a corporation, that Mr. Sharma passed away two weeks later and that the settlement failed. Mr. Nemecek also stated that his Client discovered an issue of embezzlement, that he has amended the civil complaint, that they had hoped that the Public Administrator would serve, but they declined and that his Client is still evaluating how to proceed (be appointed or have someone else serve). In addition, Mr. Nemecek stated that his Client discovered that Mr. Sharma has two Siblings in the Bay Area and that they have declined to do anything with the estate. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
